    "R1.2": "The agent fails to identify the logical inconsistency in the comparison of Table 2, where a higher relative L2 error is misinterpreted as 'substantially better' generalization.",
    "R1.3": "The agent does not check claim-evidence linkage and fails to notice that several supporting inferences are unsupported by the reported numbers.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims, but fails to mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with one drift, as the recommendation generalizes from a single architecture and Re interval to 'PINN deployments' at large.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free but with one minor regression, as the deployment recommendation may mislead practitioners who adopt the recommended recipe.",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability, as the text presents one reading of the gradient-norm diagnostic as if it were the only reading."
